articles.title: Trump’s last-minute delay: Why he was never going to obliterate Iran in the first place
articles.summary: Trump employed delay tactics to avoid following through on his pledges to relentlessly bomb Iran, following a pattern similar to his 11th-hour tariff compromises.

articles.title: Michigan woman arrested for allegedly starving, torturing disabled sister-in-law she locked in basement
articles.summary: A Michigan woman was arrested after she allegedly kept her disabled sister-in-law locked in a basement for two years and nearly starved her to death.

articles.title: South Carolina pastor, wife arrested after alleged sexual, physical abuse of foster children
articles.summary: A South Carolina pastor and his wife were arrested after a foster child reported that the pastor sexually abused them while at the couple's home.

articles.title: Man charged after allegedly threatening to kill Ohio dad 'in the name of Allah' in terrifying video encounter
articles.summary: Anthony Long allegedly told an Ohio father he would kill him "in the name of Allah" in a terrifying encounter caught on camera.

articles.title: Dem lawmaker calls for TSA to bring back shoes-off airport security policy
articles.summary: Sen. Tammy Duckworth is calling on the TSA to bring back its controversial policy requiring travelers to remove their shoes before going through security checkpoints at airports.

articles.title: Former Fort Bragg employee charged with leaking classified military information to journalist
articles.summary: Courtney Williams is accused of leaking top-secret military tactics to an investigative journalist, according to a federal complaint.

articles.title: Florida bus driver faces child neglect charges after train clips vehicle on railroad tracks
articles.summary: A Florida school bus driver is facing child neglect and reckless driving charges after her bus was clipped by a train with 29 students on board as she just narrowly made it across the tracks.

articles.title: Wisconsin couple allegedly starved six children for years, forcing them to eat mold, bugs and dog food
articles.summary: Casey and Mary Cano face child abuse charges after allegedly starving their six children and beating them with belts at their Wisconsin home for years.

articles.title: Dem Senate candidate takes swipe at Joe Rogan after refusing to disavow Hasan Piker's past comments
articles.summary: Michigan Democrat Abdul El-Sayed doubles down on campaigning with controversial streamer Hasan Piker and takes a swing at podcaster Joe Rogan.

articles.title: ‘We're coming after you': Dr Oz revokes Medicare access for LA doctor tied to $71M hospice billing
articles.summary: A California doctor was linked to nearly 2,800 Medicare patient claims across 126 hospices in 2024, according to CBS News, amid LA hospice fraud concerns.

articles.title: Hawaii doctor learns fate after prosecutors say jealousy drove him to attack wife on cliffside hike
articles.summary: Gerhardt Konig, a doctor in Hawaii, was convicted of attempted manslaughter after trying to kill his wife on a hiking trail in Oahu in a bloody cliffside confrontation.

articles.title: Child born during international flight to US sparks heated debate about citizenship, legal identity
articles.summary: A midair birth on a flight from Kingston, Jamaica, to New York raises questions and comments about birthright citizenship, U.S. airspace and the baby's nationality.

articles.title: Florence and the Machine brings Planned Parenthood on tour to offer reproductive health advice
articles.summary: Florence + the Machine announced a partnership with Planned Parenthood on Monday to offer reproductive health information at upcoming tour concert stops.

articles.summary: A father-son duo is suing a Maine school district, claiming students were not given the opportunity to recite the Pledge of Allegiance for two years.

articles.title: Jimmy Kimmel tells UCLA women's basketball team to give Trump fake national championship trophy
articles.summary: Jimmy Kimmel urged UCLA's national champion women's basketball team to give President Donald Trump a fake trophy at the White House during a late night segment.

articles.title: MSNBC host offended by Hegseth's 'we leave no man behind' statement
articles.summary: MSNBC's Lawrence O'Donnell criticizes War Secretary Pete Hegseth for using "we leave no man behind," calling it outdated and historically inaccurate.

articles.title: Dem senator ripped for 'smear' of female activist advocating for Swalwell's accusers: 'Very bad look'
articles.summary: Sen. Ruben Gallego, D-Ariz., is facing blowback after he tried to knock a woman raising sexual misconduct accusations against Eric Swalwell, prompting Democrats and conservatives to call out the move.

articles.title: Christina Haack, Heather Rae El Moussa turn heads in bikinis during glam desert family getaway
articles.summary: Christina Haack and Heather Rae El Moussa pose poolside in stylish bikinis during a blended family Easter desert vacation with their partners.

articles.summary: The Coast Guard has opened a criminal investigation and Bahamas police have arrested Brian Hooker in connection to the disappearance of his wife, Lynette Hooker, during a Bahamas boat trip.

articles.title: Scottie Scheffler's family, including 12-day-old son, enjoy Masters Par 3 Contest at Augusta
articles.summary: Scottie Scheffler brought his entire family, including 12-day-old son Remy, to the Masters Par 3 Contest at Augusta National ahead of round one.

articles.title: Missing teen case flips as ‘kidnapped’ girl found alive, built secret life as mom of 3, investigator: report
articles.summary: An Arizona girl missing since 1994 has been found alive as a married mother of three working for a private investigations firm in Springfield, Missouri, according to a report.

articles.title: Washington Post columnist argues Muslims shouldn't have to assimilate in America
articles.summary: Washington Post columnist Shadi Hamid argued Muslims don't need to assimilate in America, citing data they are civically engaged while combatting rhetoric from GOP lawmakers.

articles.title: Wave of alleged migrant murders ignites fury across US as officials warn of more carnage, crackdown needed
articles.summary: A string of killings across the U.S. involving suspects in the country illegally is driving renewed outrage and calls for sweeping policy changes.

articles.title: White House fires back at George Clooney after actor accuses Trump of threatening a war crime
articles.summary: George Clooney blasts Trump's threat to end a civilization as a potential war crime. The White House responds by mocking Clooney's movies and acting.

articles.title: Top school district slapped with complaint alleging 'elaborate system' to keep kids' gender transitions secret

articles.title: Fox News Poll: Record number say taxes are too high; government spending seen as wasteful

articles.summary: Karoline Leavitt says Trump's threat to destroy Iranian civilization was not a bluff, claiming it forced Iran to agree to a two-week ceasefire deal.

articles.summary: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, D-N.Y., plans to force another war powers vote to rein in President Donald Trump's use of the military in Iran amid a fragile ceasefire.

articles.title: Mullin weighs using airport customs as leverage against sanctuary cities
articles.summary: DHS Secretary Mullin eyes ending customs processing at sanctuary-city airports in cities like New York and Los Angeles as immigration leverage grows.

articles.summary: Sen. Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., says the Iran ceasefire deal has "troubling aspects" and calls for a diplomatic solution that stops Iran's nuclear enrichment program completely.

articles.title: Trump admin scores Minnesota court win in Medicaid fraud crackdown

articles.title: Judge blocks Trump’s push to deport Abrego Garcia, rebukes DOJ for trying to ‘dictate’ court
articles.summary: A U.S. judge blocked Abrego Garcia's removal to Liberia, for now, further delaying a 13-month case that has spanned two continents and multiple federal court districts.

articles.title: Stormy Daniels’ disgraced ex-lawyer and anti-Trump superstar Michael Avenatti moved to halfway house
articles.summary: Michael Avenatti has been moved from federal prison to a residential reentry program in California, per Bureau of Prisons records. He remains in custody.

articles.title: US Navy requesting $3B to replenish Tomahawk missiles used in Iran war
articles.summary: The U.S. Navy requests $3 billion in additional funding to replenish Tomahawk missiles depleted during the war against Iran as part of a record budget.

articles.title: Ousted AG Bondi declines Epstein deposition, but lawmakers say subpoena still holds
articles.summary: Pam Bondi will not appear for a House Oversight deposition in the Epstein probe, with the DOJ saying she was subpoenaed as attorney general and is no longer in the role.

articles.title: Leavitt rebukes media outlets running with Iranian narratives on 10 demands
articles.summary: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt rebuked media outlets for reporting Iran's publicly released 10-point plan as acceptable to the US.

articles.title: Sen Elissa Slotkin won't rule out 2028 presidential bid but says midterms come first for Democrats

articles.title: Squad member Summer Lee calls 'upper class' the 'enemy' at El-Sayed rally

articles.title: GOP Rep Randy Fine declares that deporting all illegal immigrants is the top way to make the US affordable
articles.summary: Republican Rep. Randy Fine of Florida asserts that deporting all illegal immigrants is the top move to make the United States affordable.
